author: Kawê Cezar
description: Transforms your vault's directory structures into a streamlined grid of card visuals with fallback banners and interactive metadata. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Kawê Cezar
manifest.description: Transforms your vault's directory structures into a streamlined grid of card visuals with fallback banners and interactive metadata.
manifest.name: Gallery View Dashboard

name: Gallery View Dashboard

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
## Gallery View Dashboard v3.0.2

### 🐛 Fixes
- **PDF banner fallback**: PDF files now correctly use the dedicated `Default PDF File Banner` setting instead of the note file banner
- **Delete confirmation**: Deleting assets from the context menu now shows a confirmation dialog, preventing accidental deletions
- **Settings cleanup**: Removed duplicate "Live Library Vault Tree Structure" heading

### ✨ New Features
- **YouTube video duration**: When importing from YouTube, video duration is now fetched and displayed on the card as a `⏱ 14:30` badge (requires optional YouTube Data API v3 key in settings)
- **Improved delete flow**: Always prompts for confirmation before moving assets to trash, keeping your vault safe

### 🎨 UI Improvements
- **Redesigned tree structure**: The Live Library Vault Tree Structure has been completely overhauled with:
  - Visual indentation guide lines for clearer hierarchy
  - Folders and PDFs grouped in separate sections
  - Expand All / Collapse All controls
  - Compact row design with better hover feedback
  - Item count badges on folders
- **Simplified PDF management**: PDF files in the tree no longer have individual banner overrides — they use the global `Default PDF File Banner` setting, reducing clutter

### 🧹 Removed
- **Manual Customizations Overrides** section removed — all folder banner customization is now handled through the improved Live Library Vault Tree Structure

releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 3.0.0

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
## 🐛 Bug Fixes & Code Quality Improvements

Minor patch release focusing on bug fixes, code quality improvements, and YouTube banner enhancements.

![Gallery Plugin Demo](https://github.com/kawecz/gallery-view-dashboard/raw/main/public/gifv2.gif)

---

## ✨ Improvements

### 🎬 YouTube Banner Enhancement
- **Cropped YouTube Thumbnails**: YouTube video banners now automatically crop out black letterboxing bars for a cleaner look
- Higher quality thumbnail fetching with `maxresdefault.jpg`

### 🔧 Code Quality
- **Lint Compliance**: Resolved all ESLint warnings and errors for better code maintainability
- **Type Safety**: Replaced unsafe `any` types with proper TypeScript types throughout the codebase
- **API Compliance**: Updated to use proper Obsidian APIs (`activeDocument`, `FileManager.trashFile()`, etc.)
- **CSS Best Practices**: Removed unnecessary `!important` declarations by increasing selector specificity
- **Settings Cleanup**: Removed deprecated API usage and improved settings heading labels

### 🔄 Drag & Drop Improvements
- **Manual Reorder Fix**: Fixed manual card reordering logic for more reliable drag-and-drop behavior
- Better fallback handling when no saved order exists

---

## 🐛 Bug Fixes Fixed in This Release

- Fixed manual card reorder not working consistently
- Fixed YouTube banners displaying with black bars
- Fixed unsafe type assignments causing potential runtime errors
- Fixed deprecated Obsidian API method usage
- Fixed CSS lint warnings for `!important` overuse

---

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

None. This release is fully backward compatible with v2.0.0 settings and configurations.

---

## 📦 Installation

### From Obsidian Community Plugins (Recommended)
1. Open Obsidian Settings
2. Go to Community Plugins → Browse
3. Search for "Gallery View"
4. Click Install → Enable

### Manual Installation
1. Download the latest release from GitHub
2. Extract files to `.obsidian/plugins/gallery-view/`
3. Reload Obsidian
4. Enable plugin in Community Plugins settings

---

## 🔧 Compatibility

- **Obsidian Version**: v0.15.0 or higher
- **Platforms**: Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android
- **Mobile Support**: Full mobile compatibility maintained

---

## 📋 Upgrade Notes

When upgrading from v2.0.0:
1. Your existing settings, folder sizes, and sort orders will be preserved
2. No migration steps required
3. YouTube banner cropping will apply automatically to new imports

---

## 📄 Full Changelog

### Changed
- YouTube thumbnails now use `maxresdefault.jpg` for higher quality
- YouTube banners auto-crop black bars via CSS `scale(1.15)`
- `Vault.delete()` replaced with `FileManager.trashFile()` to respect user preferences
- `document` references updated to `activeDocument` for popout window compatibility
- Settings tab heading changed from "settings" to "configurations"

### Fixed
- Manual card reorder drop handler logic
- Unsafe `any` type assignments across `main.ts`, `settings.ts`, and `view.ts`
- Promise handling in event callbacks (void IIFE wrapping)
- `setAttrs` style assignments replaced with direct style property setters
- Unnecessary escape characters in regex patterns
- `hasOwnProperty` called via `Object.prototype` for safety

### Removed
- All `!important` declarations from CSS
- Unused `isYouTube` variable
- Deprecated `display` method warnings (already properly overridden)

releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 2.0.1

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
# Gallery View Plugin v2.0.0

## 🎉 Major Update: Enhanced Grid Performance & Visual Improvements

I am excited to announce version 2.0.0 of the Gallery View Plugin! This release focuses on performance improvements, smoother rendering, and better visual consistency.

![Gallery Plugin Demo](https://github.com/kawecz/gallery-view-dashboard/raw/main/public/gifv2.gif)

---

## ✨ New Features & Improvements

### 🚀 Performance Optimizations
- **Smart Grid Refresh System**: Added `fresh-load` class mechanism that prevents visual flickering when refreshing the gallery
- **Optimized Rendering Pipeline**: Grid now properly clears and resets before loading new items, eliminating duplicate content issues
- **Memory Management**: Improved cleanup of DOM elements during navigation

### 🎨 Visual Enhancements
- **Dynamic Card Sizing**: CSS custom properties (`--card-custom-size`) now provide smoother size transitions
- **Consistent Grid Layout**: Improved grid rendering with proper CSS variable handling

### 🔧 Technical Improvements
- **Better State Management**: Enhanced grid initialization and cleanup process
- **Removed FOUC (Flash of Unstyled Content)**: The new `fresh-load` class system prevents visible layout shifts during refresh

### 🔧New Features 
- Adding a button to add notes and create notes using YouTube URL videos
- The video will be automatic integrated inside the note with an Iframe
- Added a scale size of the cards and new configurations on the config menu. 

---

## 🐛 Bug Fixes Fixed in This Release

- Fixed issue where grid content would duplicate during rapid navigation
- Resolved visual flickering when switching between folders
- Corrected CSS variable propagation for card sizes
- Fixed grid refresh timing issues

---

## ⚠️ Breaking Changes

None. This release is fully backward compatible with existing settings and configurations.

---

## 📦 Installation

### From Obsidian Community Plugins (Recommended)
1. Open Obsidian Settings
2. Go to Community Plugins → Browse
3. Search for "Gallery View"
4. Click Install → Enable

### Manual Installation
1. Download the latest release from GitHub
2. Extract files to `.obsidian/plugins/gallery-view/`
3. Reload Obsidian
4. Enable plugin in Community Plugins settings

---

## 🔧 Compatibility

- **Obsidian Version**: v0.15.0 or higher
- **Platforms**: Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, Android
- **Mobile Support**: Full mobile compatibility maintained

---

## 📋 Upgrade Notes

When upgrading from previous versions:
1. Your existing settings, folder sizes, and sort orders will be preserved
2. No migration steps required
3. Clear browser cache if experiencing any visual issues (Ctrl/Cmd + Shift + R)

---

## 🙏 Acknowledgments

Special thanks to all users who reported the grid refresh issues and provided feedback on the rendering performance.

---

## 🔜 What's Next?

- Keyboard navigation improvements
- Batch operations for multiple cards
- Custom CSS snippet support
- Enhanced drag-and-drop feedback

---

## 🐞 Report Issues

Encountered a bug? Please report it at:
[Your GitHub Issues URL]

Include:
- Obsidian version
- Plugin version (2.0.0)
- Steps to reproduce
- Screenshots/video if applicable

---

## 📄 Full Changelog

### Added
- `fresh-load` class for grid loading states
- CSS custom property `--card-custom-size` for dynamic sizing
- `grid.empty()` call before rendering new items
- `grid.removeClass("fresh-load")` after content clearing

### Changed
- Grid creation now includes inline style with CSS variable
- Improved grid refresh mechanism

### Fixed
- Grid duplication during navigation
- Visual flickering on content refresh
- CSS variable propagation issues

---

**Happy Organizing! 🎨**

*Released: June 9, 2026*
releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 2.0.0

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
## 🚀 Release v1.0.3 — Global Display Settings & Layout Polish

I am happy to push out **v1.0.3**, which focuses on extending global customization controls and hardening the live directory tree mechanics inside the plugin configuration panel. Here is a breakdown of what I've changed and introduced in this version:

![Gallery Plugin Demo](https://github.com/kawecz/gallery-view-dashboard/raw/main/public/gif.gif)

### 🌟 What's New

* **📊 Injected Folder Progress Bars Toggle Switch:** I added a new global display option to the configuration dashboard. When enabled, the plugin will recursively scan notes inside your directories and print visual completion bars if a frontmatter `checkbox` metadata key exists on your files.

### 🔧 Fixes & Optimizations

* **🛠️ Robust Vault Tree Resolution:** I refactored the underlying logic inside `displayFolderTree` to ensure that specifying nested directories (such as `conteudo` or custom deeply-nested structures) as your *Library Root Target Path* will no longer cause the configuration layout window to break or render blank containers.
* **✨ Production Layout Consistency:** I preserved the core filtering logic where your target root search path is cleanly treated as the top-level directory boundary, ensuring only the exact nested directories and native asset targets (like PDFs) populate your custom banner layout fields.
* **⚡ Smooth Interface Updates:** Cleaned up the input update logic to guarantee that toggling settings or changing paths triggers predictable interface rendering updates across active workspace leaves instantly.

---

### 📝 Core Settings Configuration Preview

```json
{
  "showFolderProgress": true,
  "visibleProperties": ["tags", "status", "todo"],
  "showCheckboxes": true
}

```

*Thank you for utilizing Gallery View! If you encounter any unexpected alignment issues with your custom banners, feel free to submit an issue or reach out directly.*
releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 1.0.3

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description: ## 🚀 Release Notes: v1.0 .2 — Drag & Drop Lock + Smart Manual Sorting This update focuses entirely on improving user experience, adding more granular control over card sorting, and resolving unexpected layout jumps when switching view modes. ### 🆕 What's New * **🎯 Smart Baseline for Manual Sorting** * Switching from **Alphabetical** to **Manual Reorder** mode no longer scrambles your cards into an arbitrary, disorganized mess. * The layout now seamlessly initializes your manual grid using your current alphabetical order as a blueprint. You can then begin dragging cards around from a clean baseline. * **🔒 Functional Sorting Lock** * Introduced a persistent **Drag & Drop Lock** toggle directly next to the sorting method dropdown. * **Locked State (Default):** Prevents accidental drag actions while navigating folders. Cards behave like standard pointer links. * **Unlocked State:** Dynamically changes the cursor to a `grab` utility and activates card reordering. * **🧹 Streamlined View Controls** * Removed the buggy "Same as Folder" external plugin lookup option to maintain a cleaner, zero-dependency codebase that compiles with strict TypeScript type-safety. --- ### 🛠️ Behind the Scenes (Technical Tweaks) * **TypeScript Optimization:** Resolved compilation flow errors (`TS18048`) where nested closures tracking `savedOrder` states inside the sorting array could evaluate to an undefined state. * **Micro-Animations:** Injected a subtle programmatic shrink-and-pop scaling transition onto the lock action state bar to give the user immediate visual feedback when toggling interactive rules. --- *Thank you for using Gallery View! If you encounter any bugs or layout regressions, feel free to open an issue or pull request.*
releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 1.0.2

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
v1.1.0 - Dynamic Card Layout & Responsive Image Support 🚀

This release completely re-engineers how library asset cards handle images across different viewport scales. Instead of locking image banners to static, hardcoded aspect ratios, the UI now dynamically adapts to the natural dimensions of your images while remaining completely responsive.

![Gallery Plugin Demo](https://github.com/kawecz/gallery-view-dashboard/raw/main/public/gif.gif)


🛠️ What's Changed
📐 Fully Dynamic Image-Driven Card Layout

    Removed restrictive fixed-height constraints (height: 140px) and absolute position overlays from the card asset covers.

    The image canvas wrapper now automatically calculates and adapts its height dynamically to the natural, intrinsic dimensions of your banner graphics.

    Masonry-style Desktop Alignment: In desktop viewports, cards maintain row consistency with clean min-content row tracks while giving images room to present their true dimensions without uneven stretching.

📱 Perfect Mobile Optimization

    Fixed an issue where high-width scaling on mobile screen viewports distorted or crushed card layouts.

    Implemented fluid mobile flex scaling, ensuring that cards up to max-width: 280px adapt cleanly, maintaining pristine graphic aspect ratios on smartphones and small panels.

💻 Technical File Adjustments Breakdown

    view.ts: Cleaned up element construction to strip inline positioning overrides, relying on clean stylesheets.

    styles.css:

        Reconfigured .gallery-view-grid to manage cards using a flexible Masonry approach via grid-auto-rows: min-content and align-items: start.

        Updated .gallery-view-card-banner-wrap and .gallery-view-banner-img to use fluid auto-height bounds.

        Added responsive @media view breakpoints to dynamically handle mobile display layout scaling.

📥 Installation / Update

    Download the updated main.js, manifest.json, and styles.css from this release.

    Replace the older assets inside your vault folder directory (.obsidian/plugins/your-plugin-id/).

    Reload or reactivate the plugin within Obsidian settings! Enjoy your beautiful, dynamic asset library gallery view.
releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard

releases.author: kawecz
releases.description:
📝 Release v1.0.0 - Official Initial Launch

We are thrilled to introduce Gallery View Dashboard, a powerful new way to visualize and navigate your Obsidian vault. This plugin transforms your directory structures into a streamlined, card-based interface, making your knowledge library more accessible and beautiful than ever before.

![Gallery Plugin Demo](https://github.com/kawecz/gallery-view-dashboard/raw/main/public/gif.gif)

✨ Key Features

    Visual Library Interface: Transform any vault folder into a beautiful, card-based dashboard with a single click.

    Hierarchical Navigation: Seamlessly browse between subfolders and files with an intuitive navigation system.

    Customizable Banners: Set unique banner images for folders and individual notes to personalize your dashboard.

    Live Settings Sync: Instant updates to your library structure—no need to reload the plugin when changing your root path.

    Smart Folder Suggestion: Easily configure your library paths with an integrated auto-complete suggestion system.

    Task Management: Toggle checkboxes directly from the gallery view to keep your workflows moving.

🐛 Improvements & Fixes

    Optimized rendering engine for faster performance when traversing deep folder trees.

    Fixed the folder override rendering logic to ensure a cleaner, isolated management experience.

    Resolved UI inconsistencies during dynamic path changes.

    Improved cross-platform compatibility for folder path handling.

🚀 How to Install

    Open Obsidian and go to Settings > Community Plugins.

    Ensure Restricted Mode is disabled.

    Click Browse and search for Gallery View Dashboard.

    Click Install and then Enable.

Built with care by [Kawê Cezar](https://github.com/kawecz). If you enjoy this plugin, consider giving the repository a star!
releases.name: Gallery View Dashboard 1.0.0
